code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-MAR-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), and urine analysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible renal pathology or urinary tract issues. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, raising concern for hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic status, and possible underlying causes for abnormal urine findings.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
